author: Augustine of Hippo
book: A Treatise on the Predestination of the Saints.

classification.cautionReason: No major mainstream heresy warning is attached by the preview classifier.

lineage: A Treatise on the Predestination of the Saints.
A Treatise on the Gift of Perseverance.
Why God Willed that He Should Be Asked for that Which He Might Give Without Prayer.
originalBook: A Treatise on the Gift of Perseverance.
title: Why God Willed that He Should Be Asked for that Which He Might Give Without Prayer.

verses.book: A Treatise on the Predestination of the Saints.
verses.text: Wherefore, also He willed that He should be asked that we may not be led into temptation, because if we are not led, we by no means depart from Him.

verses.book: A Treatise on the Predestination of the Saints.
verses.text: And this might have been given to us even without our praying for it, but by our prayer He willed us to be admonished from whom we receive these benefits.

verses.book: A Treatise on the Predestination of the Saints.
verses.text: For from whom do we receive but from Him from whom it is right for us to ask?

verses.book: A Treatise on the Predestination of the Saints.
verses.text: Truly in this matter let not the Church look for laborious disputations, but consider its own daily prayers.

verses.book: A Treatise on the Predestination of the Saints.
verses.text: It prays that the unbelieving may believe; therefore God converts to the faith.

verses.book: A Treatise on the Predestination of the Saints.
verses.text: It prays that believers may persevere; therefore God gives perseverance to the end.

verses.book: A Treatise on the Predestination of the Saints.
verses.text: God foreknew that He would do this.

verses.book: A Treatise on the Predestination of the Saints.
verses.text: This is the very predestination of the saints, “whom He has chosen in Christ before the foundation of the world, that they should be holy and unspotted before Him in love; predestinating them unto the adoption of children by Jesus Christ to Himself, according to the good pleasure of His will, to the praise of the glory of His grace, in which He hath shown them favour in His beloved Son, in whom they have redemption through His blood, the forgiveness of sins according to the riches of His grace, which has abounded towards them in all wisdom and prudence; that He might show them the mystery of His will according to His good pleasure which He hath purposed in Him, in the dispensation of the fulness of times to restore all things in Christ which are in heaven and which are in earth; in Him, in whom also we have obtained a lot, being predestinated according to His purpose who worketh all things.”

verses.book: A Treatise on the Predestination of the Saints.
verses.text: Against a trumpet of truth so clear as this, what man of sober and watchful faith can receive any human arguments?
